event: Portrait of St. Pierre Pascal, Bishop of Jaen
symbols: angel offering a crown — the crown of martyrdom and heavenly reward
book and pen — the bishop's teaching office and theological writings
bishop's mitre on the table — his episcopal dignity set aside in humility
white vestments — the Mercedarian order's garb and purity of witness
rosary at his belt — devotional prayer life
description: A full-length portrait of an elderly bearded man in white Mercedarian vestments standing against a dark background. He holds an open book in his left hand and appears to write with his right. Above him at upper left, an angel in a red garment descends, offering a leafy crown. At lower left, a table holds a white bishop's mitre and a dark object (possibly a book or hat). The figure has a serene, upward-gazing expression. A rosary hangs from his belt.
theological_description: Zurbaran's portrait depicts Saint Pierre Pascal (Pedro Pascual, c. 1227-1300), a Mercedarian friar who became Bishop of Jaen and was martyred by the Moors in Granada. The angel offering a crown connects to the biblical promise of martyrs' reward: 'Be faithful, even to the point of death, and I will give you life as your victor's crown' (Revelation 2:10). Pierre Pascal devoted himself to ransoming Christian captives from Muslim captivity — a mission rooted in Christ's own declaration: 'I have come to set the captives free' (Luke 4:18, citing Isaiah 61:1). His book and pen identify him as a theologian and writer who used the Word to defend the faith. The bishop's mitre set aside recalls Jesus's teaching on servant leadership: 'The greatest among you will be your servant' (Matthew 23:11). His white Mercedarian habit symbolizes the order's mission of mercy — 'Blessed are the merciful, for they will be shown mercy' (Matthew 5:7). Pierre Pascal ultimately gave his life for the faith, joining the great company of martyrs who 'did not love their lives so much as to shrink from death' (Revelation 12:11).

scripture_refs.reason: The crown of life promised to those faithful unto death — the angel bearing the martyr's crown
Setting captives free — the Mercedarian mission of ransoming prisoners that Pierre Pascal embodied
The greatest as servant — the bishop's mitre set aside in humility
Those who overcame by not loving their lives unto death — Pierre Pascal's martyrdom

_meta.title: St. Pierre Pascal, Bishop of Jaen
